description: Add a search/filter input at the top of the sidebar navigation in App.tsx that filters menu items as the user types. Since there are 50+ nav items, this helps users quickly find the page they need.
steps: In frontend/src/App.tsx, inside the SidebarContent component, add a search input between the role badge section and the nav items scrollable area. The input should: (a) be always visible (not inside the scroll container), (b) have a placeholder like 'Search menu...', (c) update a local state variable (searchQuery), (d) include a clear (X) button that appears when text is entered.

Filter the NAV_ALL items: when searchQuery is non-empty, only show nav items whose label matches the query (case-insensitive). Section headers should be hidden when filtering. When searchQuery is empty, show all items as normal.
Style the search input to match the dark sidebar theme: dark background, light text, subtle border, appropriate padding. Should not show when sidebar is collapsed (isDesktop && collapsed).
Ensure the search still works on mobile/tablet drawer mode.
acceptance_criteria: A search input is visible at the top of the sidebar navigation area
Typing in the search input filters nav items to only show matching ones
Clear button resets the filter and shows all items
Section headers are hidden when search is active
Search input is not visible when sidebar is collapsed on desktop
verification: Run 'cd /projects/sandbox/jubilant-waddle/frontend && npm run build' - should succeed
